Watched it and I absolutely hated it 😢
It felt like an incoherent mess with all the different storylines and side characters, the music I simply wasn't a fan of (I understand the soundtrack could be for some people, just wasn't for me) and the whole scene with the baby was just... Ughhh. It disturbed me
Jenna Ortega wasn't bad, Monica Belluccis and Willem Dafoe's characters felt very kinda bland, they didn't have much going on
And one thing I found odd was that there was no mention of Otho. There was mention of Charles and Maxie Dean and the Maitland's but no OTHO
Overall, I'm sad I didn't enjoy the film and I wouldn't watch it again unfortunately

At 36 years, Beetlejuice is now tied with Top Gun for the biggest gap, that I know of, between a movie and its sequel with at least one original actor returning.

As for a certain scene in the film that is not in the trailer. Maria (Skłodowska) Curie was a Polish woman who moved to France and made a career there. Don't make her out to be a French leftist and feminist, because feminism meant something else back then. A modern feminist wouldn't take her husband's last name and wouldn't achieve as much as Maria.

I've been waiting for this day for what feels like an eternity! Tim Burton's Beetlejuice Beetlejuice is finally here, and it's everything I've hoped for and more. The original film was a masterpiece of gothic comedy, and this sequel manages to capture that same magic while also adding a fresh layer of humor and heart. Michael Keaton is as brilliant as ever as Beetlejuice, the titular demon with a penchant for mischief and mayhem. His chemistry with Winona Ryder is as strong as ever, and it's a delight to see them back together on screen. The new cast members, including Justin Theroux, Monica Bellucci, and Jenna Ortega, fit seamlessly into the world of Beetlejuice.
The film's visuals are stunning, and Burton's signature style is on full display. The costumes, sets, and special effects are all top-notch, and they help to create a truly immersive experience. Danny Elfman's score is perfect, and it adds to the film's eerie and atmospheric feel.
But what really sets Beetlejuice Beetlejuice apart is its heart. It's a film about family, loss, and the power of love. It's a film that will make you laugh, cry, and think. It's a film that will stay with you long after the credits roll.
If you're a fan of the original Beetlejuice or just a fan of great filmmaking, I highly recommend checking out this sequel. It's a must-see.

Definitely a very piecemeal plot. As much as Burton still is inventive, he should have had his screenwriters keep more with the simplicity of the original movie. Movie plots have become too much navel-gazing and revolved around show business and it seems a lot of show business has lost touch with the ordinary world. But as special effects spectacles go, it’s definitely more fun than certainly many of the superhero movies of the last few years have been. I never had much faith in a Beetlejuice movie sequel. But if this is the way they were going to approach one, it’s definitely entertaining.
